# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// src/middlewares/authMiddleware.ts
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next';  // Import necessary types for request handling in NodeJS server-side environment (Nextjs API Routes)
const AUTH_COOKIE = process.env.AUTH_COOKIE;   // Get the auth cookie name stored on env variables or hardcoded value as per requirement, example: "authToken" 
// Check if request contains an authorization header with a Bearer token (usually in combination of 'Bearer' and user-provided access tokens) then assume that it is authenticated. Otherwise we return unauthenticated response to all other requests for security reasons as the middleware should not be called on non-authentication paths
const checkAuthed = async(request: NextRequest): Promise<NextResponse> => {  // Define a function which will handle checking authentication token in request headers and if necessary, returning unauthenticated response.  
    const cookieStore = await request.cookies();                          // Get all cookies from the current session (authentication) of user    
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ message: 'You are not authenticated' }), { status: 401, headers: {} })                                                             ;   if (!AUTH_COOKIE in cookieStore ){                   // If auth token is missing (not set) then user isn’t logged-in
        return new Response(JSON.stringify({ message:'You are not authenticated' }), { status : 401, headers: {} })    ;   };     else if (!cookieStore[AUTH_COOKIE]){                     // If auth cookie is missing (not set) then user isn’t logged-in
            return new Response(JSON.stringify({ message:'You are not authenticated' }), { status : 401, headers: {} })    ;   };     else if (!Buffer.isBuffer(cookieStore[AUTH_COOKIE].start) || cookieStore[AUTH_COOKIE].start !== process.env['TOKEN_SECRET']){ // If auth token is not matching or has expired
                return new Response(JSON.stringify({ message:'You are not authenticated' }), { status : 401, headers: {} })    ;   };     else{                                                  // User successfully logged in with valid credentials so we can allow request to proceed using response object returned from this function call
                return NextResponse.next();                              }}                                                                                     });                                  */;                             /* Make sure that the user is not authenticated by default and then check for auth token presence on every route hit as per requirement, otherwise deny requests (return 401)*/}});    // Export middleware function which we can use in server.ts
